claims.claim: In an oven test for consumer batteries, an oven temperature of 150 °C is used (UL2054).
claims.quote:
For consumer batteries, an oven tempera-
 ture of 150 8C is used (UL2054).

claims.claim: In a short-circuit abuse test, a low resistance less than 5 mΩ is connected across the battery terminals.
claims.quote:
Short-circuit: A low resistance (<5 mO) is connected
across the terminals of the battery.

claims.claim: In a nail abuse test, a nail may be forced through the battery at a prescribed rate such as 8 cm/s.
claims.quote:
Nail: A nail is forced through the battery at a prescribed
rate (such as 8 cm/s).

claims.claim: The SEI layer can decompose exothermically at 90–120 °C.
claims.quote:
This layer is metastable and can decompose
exothermically at 90–120 8C.

claims.claim: At elevated temperatures greater than 120 °C, the SEI layer does not protect the negative electrode from contact with the electrolyte, and an exothermic reaction between intercalated lithium and electrolyte can occur.
claims.quote:
At
elevated temperatures (>120 8C), the SEI layer does not
protect the negative electrode from contact with the
electrolyte and an exothermic reaction between inter-
calated lithium and electrolyte can occur.

claims.claim: Under normal battery operating conditions, electrolyte tends to be oxidized/reduced on the surface of the highly delithiated cathode/fully lithiated anode, leading to gas production, especially in the formation cycles.
claims.quote:
Under  normal  battery  operating
conditions,  electrolyte  tends  to  be  oxidized/educed  on  the
surface  of  the  highly  delithiated  cathode/fully  lithiated  anode,
leading  to  gas  production,  especially  in  the  formation  cycles.

claims.claim: Gases production is fully enhanced under thermal runaway conditions, including flammable gases such as O2, C2H4, and C2H6.
claims.quote:
Furthermore,  gases  production  will  be  fully
enhanced under thermal runaway conditions, including a large
number of flammable gases, like O2, C2H4, and C2H6et al.

claims.claim: Overheating is a trigger for thermal runaway, which occurs from overcharging, overheating, external short circuits, or internal short circuits, resulting in fast increase of battery internal temperature.
claims.quote:
Overheating is a trigger for the thermal runaway, which occurs
from  overcharging,[8b,10]  overheating,[4a,11]  external  short  cir-
cuits[12] or internal short circuits,[13] resulting in fast increase of
battery internal temperature.

claims.claim: Li2CO3 tends to decompose into CO2 based on the reaction 2Li2CO3→4Li+ + 4e− + 2CO2 + 1O2 at a relative low voltage of 3.8 V.
claims.quote:
found that it tends to decompose into CO2 based on the decom-
position reaction of 2Li2CO3→4Li+ + 4e− + 2CO2 + 1O2 at a rela-
tive  low  voltage  of  3.8  V

claims.claim: The reaction mechanism between singlet oxygen and ethylene carbonate (EC) was proposed as 21O2 + EC → 2CO2 + CO + H2O.
claims.quote:
the  corre-
sponding  reaction  equation  can  be  described  as  21O2  +  EC  → 
2CO2  +  CO  +  H2O.

claims.claim: In penetration-induced internal short circuit, no current flows through connecting tabs when the battery is completely penetrated and each stacked electrode layer is short-circuited by a metal nail.
claims.quote: When a battery is completely penetrated, each stacked electrode layer is short-circuited by a metal nail, thus forming a closed-loop path between each layer of electrodes. In this case, no current flows though connecting tabs

claims.claim: The typical puncture speed in penetration failure is 8 cm/s.
claims.quote:
The typical 

puncture speed is 8 cm/s

claims.claim: For batteries with a thickness of 1–2 cm, the complete puncture time is less than 1 s in most cases.
claims.quote:
for batteries with a thickness of 1–2 cm, the 
complete puncture time is less than 1s in most cases

claims.claim: In nail penetration, the contact resistance is usually several orders of magnitude larger than the nail internal resistance, so the total resistance is dominated by the contact resistance.
claims.quote:
In the nail pene-
tration  scenario,  the  contact  resistance  is  usually  several  orders  of
magnitude larger than the nail internal resistance, so the total resistance
is dominated by the contact resistance

claims.claim: LiPF6 salt decomposes at 107 °C and generates HF.
claims.quote:
LiPF6 salt decomposes at 107 

◦

C and generates HF:

claims.quote:
When the internal pressure
of the battery exceeds the critical safety pressure of the pressure-relief
port, the gas will be forced out of the pressure-relief port by the inter-
nal pressure

claims.quote:
The LIBs eruption process is usually divided into two stages: initial
and  main  eruption

claims.quote:
SEI  layer
breaks down at a temperature between 80 and 120
C in the battery,
releasing reducing gases including H2  and  C2H4

claims.quote:
It was found that

temperature of 140
C.

claims.quote:
maintain the battery pack within the optimal temperature range of 20

C with a temperature difference (ΔT) of less than

C

claims.quote:
The proposed BTMS controls the maximum temperature at 43.83
C with a discharge rate
of 3C

claims.quote:
which is reduced by 20.13
C compared to the traditional system with a solid aluminum heat sink at the
same mass flow.

claims.claim: After four continuous 3C discharge and charge cycles, the highest battery temperature is 43.90 °C and the temperature difference is 3.46 °C.
claims.quote:
The highest battery temperature is 43.90
C with a temperature difference of
3.46
C, respectively, after four continuous 3C discharge and charge cycles.

claims.claim: When the maximum flow rate of the coolant is 0.12 m/s, the maximum Reynolds number for water is 477.7.
claims.quote:
when the maximum flow rate of the coolant is 0.12 m/s,
the maximum Reynolds number for water is 477.7.

claims.claim: The ideal work temperature range is 20–50 °C, and the temperature difference should be kept within 5 °C.
claims.quote:
control the  battery  temperature  during  the  ideal  work  temperature  range  of  20–50 

C, and keep the temperature difference within 5 

C

claims.claim: The battery module consists of 16 lithium-ion 18,650 batteries with nominal capacity 2.0 Ah, nominal voltage 3.7 V, and internal resistance 45 mΩ.
claims.quote:
the  battery  module  consists  of  16  lithium-ion 

batteries  of  type  18,650  with  nominal  capacity  of  2.0  Ah,  nominal 

voltage of 3.7 V and internal resistance of 45 mΩ.

claims.claim: The overall size of the BTMS is 97 × 97 × 65 mm.
claims.quote:
The overall size of BTMS is 

97 × 97 × 65 mm.

claims.claim: The PCM used is paraffin RT44HC with melting temperature of 41–44 °C.
claims.quote:
The PCM used in this study is paraffin RT44HC with melting tem-

perature of 41–44 

C

claims.claim: The initial condition is t = 0, T = T0, v = v0 and P = 0, where T0 is set as 40 °C and v0 is set as 0.01 m/s.
claims.quote:
The initial condition is as: t = 0, T = T0, v = v0 and P = 0, where T0 is 

set as 40 

C and v0  is set as 0.01 m/s.

claims.claim: Thermal-runaway retardant (TRR) is defined as a chemical that can rapidly terminate exothermic reactions in a lithium-ion battery.
claims.quote:
TRR is
a chemical that can rapidly terminate exothermic reactions in LIB.

claims.claim: When the temperature rises to above 90 °C, a series of exothermic electrochemical reactions and chemical decompositions takes place, and thermal runaway occurs.
claims.quote:
When the temperature rises to above 90 (cid:3)C, a series of
exothermic electrochemical reactions and chemical decom-
positions takes place, and thermal runaway occurs.

claims.claim: In a nail penetration test on coin cells, about 4 wt.% pentadecane reduced the maximum temperature by about 60%.
claims.quote:
In nail
penetration test on coin cells, (cid:2)4 wt. % pentadecane reduced the maximum temperature by (cid:2)60%;

claims.claim: In an impact test on pouch cells, about 5 wt.% pentadecane reduced the maximum temperature by about 90%.
claims.quote: in impact test on pouch cells, (cid:2)5 wt. % pentadecane reduced the maximum temperature by (cid:2)90%.

claims.claim: The diffusion rate of pentadecane in the electrode layer stack was measured to be about 580 μm/s.
claims.quote:
The diffusion rate of pentadecane in the electrode layer stack was measured
to be (cid:2)580 lm/s.

claims.claim: Fire incidents during charging sessions account for approximately 26% of all cases.
claims.quote: fire incidents during  charging  sessions  account  for  approximately  26%  of  all  cases

claims.claim: Fires occurring while vehicles are parked represent about 13% of incidents.
claims.quote:
fires  occurring  while
vehicles are parked represent a significant portion (about 13%) of incidents

claims.claim: Thermal runaway refers to a phenomenon where a chain exothermic reaction occurs within a lithium-ion battery under mechanical, thermal, electrical, and other triggers, with constant heat accumulation leading to battery damage and triggering battery combustion or explosion.
claims.quote:
Thermal runaway refers to the phenomenon in which a chain exothermic reaction occurs within
the  lithium-ion  battery  under  mechanical,  thermal,  electrical,  and  other  triggers,  and  the  constant
accumulation of heat leads to damage to the battery, thus triggering a battery combustion or explosion.

claims.claim: The triggers of thermal runaway in Li-ion batteries include mechanical abuse (crushing and collision), electrical abuse (overcharging and internal short circuits), and thermal abuse.
claims.quote:
The triggers of thermal runaway in Li-ion batteries are mechanical abuse due to crushing and collision,
electrical  abuse  due  to  overcharging  and  internal  short  circuits,  and  thermal  abuse.

claims.claim: Yang et al. showed that aligned arrangement has 26.1% higher cooling efficiency and better thermal uniformity than staggered arrangement.
claims.quote:
Yang et al. investigated the effect of different arrangements of
cells on the temperature and showed that aligned arrangement has 26.1% higher cooling efficiency
and better thermal uniformity than staggered arrangement [14].

claims.claim: Current collector accounts for more than 90% of the electric conductivity and approximately 90% of the mechanical strength of the electrode in lithium-ion batteries.
claims.quote:
Current collector accounts for more than 90% of the electric conductivity and (cid:2)90% of the
mechanical strength of the electrode in lithium-ion battery (LIB).

claims.claim: When local temperature at a shorting site reaches approximately 110 °C, secondary reactions (including decomposition of electrolyte and electrodes) would be activated.
claims.quote:
When T reaches (cid:2)110 (cid:3)C, a number of secondary
reactions would be activated, including the decomposition of
electrolyte and electrodes.

claims.claim: The cathode and the anode in lithium-ion batteries are separated by a thin porous polymer film of 10–30 μm thickness.
claims.quote:
separated by a thin porous polymer ﬁlm of 10–30 lm thick-
ness.

claims.claim: In cycling tests, the experiment was conducted at 1C rate from 3 V to 4.3 V.
claims.quote:
The cycling experiment was conducted at
1C rate from 3 V to 4.3 V.

claims.claim: The peak temperature increase of the reference cell (DTref) is approximately 5 °C, reached in about half a minute.
claims.quote:
The
peak temperature increase of the reference cell, DTref, is
(cid:2)5 (cid:3)C, which is reached in about half a minute, followed by
a plateau wherein the heat generation and heat transfer are
balanced.

claims.claim: Hill’48 criterion is commonly used to describe orthotropy of the material when building the constitutive model.
claims.quote: Hill’48 criterion is commonly used to describe the orthotropy of the material when building the constitutive model

claims.claim: Hill’48 criterion is expressed as: 2fHill ≡ F(σ22 − σ33)2 + G(σ33 − σ11)2 + H(σ11 − σ22)2 + 2Lσ23^2 + 2Mσ13^2 + 2Nσ12^2 = 1.
Yan et al. stated that four aspects must be considered when establishing the separator constitutive model: orthotropy, viscoelasticity, temperature dependence, and thermal shrinkage properties.
claims.quote:
Yan  et  al.
pointed  out  that the  following  four  aspects must  be considered  when
establishing the constitutive model of the separator: (1) Orthotropy; (2)
Viscoelasticity;  (3)  Temperature  dependence;  (4)  Thermal  shrinkage
properties

claims.claim: The separator is a porous membrane that separates the anode from the cathode while supporting the flow of electrolytes.
claims.quote:
The separator is a porous membrane that separates the anode from
the cathode while supporting the flow of electrolytes.

claims.claim: In the nail penetration test of the pouch battery, some cells undergo thermal runaway at a preset load level, while others maintain a safe state even when the load is much higher than the expected value.
claims.quote:
in
the nail penetration test of the pouch battery, some cells undergo ther-
mal runaway at a preset load level, while others maintain a safe state
even when the load is much higher than the expected value

claims.claim: Thermal runaway (TR) refers to a self-sustaining, uncontrollable increase in temperature within the battery, leading to a cascade of chemical reactions and the release of large amounts of heat and gas.
claims.quote:
TR refers to a self-sustaining, uncontrollable increase in temperature 
within the battery, leading to a cascade of chemical reactions and the 
release of large amounts of heat and gas.

claims.claim: The TR temperature response is commonly described using three distinct temperatures [T1, T2 and T3].
claims.quote:
The TR temperature response is commonly described using three distinct 
temperatures [T1, T2 and T3] [31,32]:

claims.claim: T1 signifies the temperature at which detectable self-heating of the battery begins due to solid electrolyte interface (SEI) decomposition.
claims.quote:
T1 signifies the temperature at 
which detectable self-heating  of  the  battery  begins  due  to  solid  elec-
trolyte interface (SEI) decomposition.

claims.claim: T2 represents the temperature at which thermal runaway is triggered, often associated with separator failure leading to an internal short circuit (ISC).
claims.quote:
T2 represents the temperature at 
which TR is triggered, often associated with separator failure leading to 
an internal short circuit (ISC).

claims.claim: T3 designates the peak temperature of thermal runaway and is identifiable as the highest point on the temperature profile.
claims.quote:
T3 designates the peak 
temperature of TR and is identifiable as the highest point on the tem-
perature  profile.

claims.claim: Thermal runaway is defined as a phenomenon in which an exothermic chain reaction induces an uncontrollable increase in a cell’s temperature.
claims.quote:
Thermal runaway is a 
phenomenon in which an exothermic chain reaction induces an uncontrollable increase in the temperature of a cell.

claims.claim: The pack used in this study comprised 192 NMC811 18650 cells with a total rated energy of 2073.6 Wh.
claims.quote: comprising a group of 192 NMC811 battery cells in 18,650 format with a total rated energy of 2073.6 Wh.

claims.claim: In the decomposition-of-SEI side reaction, SEI film decomposition occurred when cell temperature exceeded 363.15 K.
claims.quote: When the temperature of the cell exceeded 363.15 K, the SEI film decomposed via an exothermic reaction.

claims.claim: In the negative electrode–electrolyte side reaction, an exothermic reaction was initiated when cell temperature exceeded 393.15 K.
claims.quote:
When the temperature of the cell exceeded 393.15 K, an exothermic reaction was initiated between the negative electrode and the 
electrolyte.

claims.claim: In the positive electrode–electrolyte side reaction, an exothermic reaction was initiated when cell temperature exceeded 443.15 K.
claims.quote:
When the temperature of the cell exceeded 443.15 K, an exothermic reaction was initiated between the positive electrode and 
electrolyte.

claims.claim: The study used a Panasonic NCR 18,650–B NCA 18,650 lithium-ion battery with NCA/graphite electrodes.
claims.quote: The battery used in this study is a widely commercialized NCA 18,650 lithium-ion battery, namely, the Panasonic NCR 18,650–B, which possesses NCA/graphite as electrodes.

claims.claim: The nominal capacity of the battery is 3350 mAh.
claims.quote: The nominal capacity of this battery is 3350 mAh,

claims.claim: The charge/discharge cutoff voltages of the battery are 4.2 V and 2.5 V, respectively.
claims.quote: and its charge/discharge cutoﬀ voltages are 4.2 and 2.5 V, respectively.

claims.claim: In the propagation tests, one battery in each pack was penetrated by a 2 mm diameter needle with a 6 mm/min loading rate.
claims.quote: During the tests, one of the batteries in each pack was penetrated by a 2 mm diameter needle with a 6 mm/min loading rate.

claims.claim: In the model, the ISC triggering condition was set to when the jellyroll node temperature reaches the separator melting point (165 °C for the chosen battery).
claims.quote: The ISC triggering condition is set as when the temperature of the jellyroll node reaches the separator melting point [39, 40] (165 °C for the chosen battery).

claims.claim: The onset temperature of thermal runaway (T2) is defined as the temperature at which the battery temperature rate exceeds 100.00 °C/min.
claims.quote:
The onset temperature  of  TR  (T2)  is
defined as the  temperature at which the battery  temperature rate ex-
◦
ceeds 100 
C/min.

claims.claim: In EV+ARC adiabatic thermal runaway tests, the system enters "Exotherm" mode when the battery temperature rate is higher than 0.02 °C/min in the "Seek" mode.
claims.quote:
When the battery temperature rate was
◦
C/min in the "Seek" mode, the EV+ARC system would
higher than 0.02 
enter the "Exotherm" mode

claims.claim: For the tested battery under 100% SoC, T1 = 86.8 °C, T2 = 252.7 °C, T3 = 995.8 °C, and dT/dt_max = 8640 °C/min.
The tested battery had a nominal capacity of 50.00 Ah and a nominal voltage of 3.65 V.

Capacity
50.00 Ah
202.8 Wh kg
3.65V

claims.quote:
The initial target temperature of
EV+ARC was set to 40 
C, the end temperature was set to 300 
C, the
◦
◦

claims.quote: The battery studied in this work is a commercial LiCoO2 (LCO)/graphite pouch battery;

claims.claim: The coupled model consists of three modules: a mechanical model, an electrochemical model, and a thermal model.
claims.quote: The coupled model consists of three modules: a mechanical model, an electrochemical model, and a thermal model

claims.claim: The relationship between compression strain and separator porosity was fitted as p* = p0 + 0.05118εz − 0.00858εz^2.
claims.quote:
can  be  fitted  by  the  function  p∗ = p0 + 0.05118εz (cid:0)
0.00858εz
2

claims.claim: A short circuit is activated when the separator strain in the thickness direction exceeds the failure strain (εz > εf).
claims.quote:
Then, once the strain-based failure criterion of the separator (given in
Eq. (2)) is satisfied, the electrical short circuit is activated:

εz > εf

claims.claim: The model contains 17,520 mesh elements, including 8100 hexahedral elements and 9420 tetrahedral elements.
claims.quote:
The
model  contains  17,520  mesh  elements,  including
8100 hexahedral elements and 9420 tetrahedral elements.

claims.claim: Experiments used 2000 mAh commercial pouch lithium-ion batteries with dimensions 44.13 mm × 32.87 mm × 9.54 mm and weight 33.89 ± 1.2 g.
claims.quote:
Experiments  were  conducted  using  2000  mAh  commercial  pouch
LIBs, with the dimensions of 44.13 mm × 32.87 mm × 9.54 mm, weight
of 33.89 ± 1.2 g

claims.claim: Battery positive active material was Li (Ni5/10Co2/10Mn3/10) O2 and the negative electrode was graphite.
claims.quote:
Li (Ni5/10Co2/10Mn3/10) O2 as the positive active
material and graphite as the negative electrode

claims.claim: Batteries were charged to 100% SOC using Neware CT-4008 at 0.5C (0.5C–1 A) current rate.
claims.quote:
using Neware CT-4008
at 0.5C (0.5C–1 A) current rate to 100% SOC.

claims.claim: The squeezing pressures applied were 1000 N, 2000 N, 3000 N, 4000 N, 5000 N, 10000 N, and 15000 N.
claims.quote:
The squeezing pressures were 1000 N, 2000 N, 3000
N, 4000
N, 5000 N, 10000 N,
and 15000 N.

claims.claim: In the extrusion state, battery thickness slowly decreases with increasing temperature and drops abruptly at thermal runaway onset.
claims.quote:
The thickness of the battery in the extrusion state slowly decreased
with increasing temperature, and dropped abruptly at the TR onset.

claims.claim: Thermal runaway of an NCM811 lithium-ion battery triggered by nail penetration was tested under three cases: full depth@100%SOC, half depth@100%SOC, and full depth@50%SOC.
claims.quote:
The thermal runaway (TR) of NCM811 Lithium-ion battery (LIB) triggered by nail penetration was tested under three cases 
of full depth@100%SOC, half depth@100%SOC, and full depth@50%SOC, respectively.

claims.claim: In the case of full depth@100%SOC, the internal peak temperature was 887.3 °C and the maximum temperature difference between the internal and surface was 256.3 °C.
claims.quote:
In the case of full depth@100%SOC and full depth@50%SOC, the internal peak temperature is 887.3 °C and 483.2 °C, 
the maximum temperature difference between the internal and surface is 256.3 °C and 92.9 °C

claims.claim: In the case of full depth@50%SOC, the internal peak temperature was 483.2 °C and the maximum temperature difference between the internal and surface was 92.9 °C.
claims.quote:
In the case of full depth@100%SOC and full depth@50%SOC, the internal peak temperature is 887.3 °C and 483.2 °C, 
the maximum temperature difference between the internal and surface is 256.3 °C and 92.9 °C

claims.claim: The maximum jet angle was 72.0° for full depth@100%SOC and 47.9° for full depth@50%SOC.
claims.quote:
the maximum jet angle is 
72.0° and 47.9°

claims.claim: The jet area duty ratio was 41.2% for full depth@100%SOC and 17.8% for full depth@50%SOC.
claims.quote: the jet area duty ratio is 41.2% and 17.8%

claims.claim: The pouch cell experiences four processes during nail penetration: nail penetration, internal short circuit, chemical reaction, and thermal runaway.
claims.quote: It is observed that the pouch cell experiences four processes including nail penetration, internal short circuit, chemical reaction and thermal runaway during nail penetration.

claims.claim: When cell temperatures exceed 506.15 K, all cells will get into thermal runaway.
claims.quote: When the temperatures of the cells exceed 506.15 K, all cells will get into thermal runaway

claims.claim: The cathode is composed of 70% lithium cobalt oxide and 30% ternary material.
claims.quote: The cathode is composed of 70% lithium cobalt oxide and 30% ternary material,

claims.claim: The nominal capacity of the cell is 1000 mAh.
claims.quote: The nominal capacity of the cell is 1000 mAh,

claims.claim: All experiments were conducted at room temperature (298.15 K).
claims.quote:
Besides, all experiments were conducted at room tempera-
ture (298.15 K).

claims.claim: The batteries tested were cylindrical with 18 mm diameter and 65 mm height.
claims.quote: The batteries used in the experimental work are cylindrical with 18 mm  diameter  and  65  mm  high.

claims.claim: The battery form factor was cylindrical 18650.
claims.quote:
Form Factor
Cylindrical 18650

claims.claim: The battery specifications included a rated capacity of 2.5 Ah, nominal voltage of 3.7 V, maximum voltage of 4.2 V, and minimum voltage of 2.8 V.
claims.quote:
Rated capacity (Ah)
2.5

3.7

4.2

claims.claim: Temperature measurements used type-K Inconel sheathed thermocouples recorded at 1 Hz with a resolution of ±0.001 °C.
claims.quote:
All  temperature  measurements  were  conducted  by  using  type-K 
Inconel  sheathed  thermocouples.  The  data  was  recorded  at  a  fre-
quency of 1 Hz with a resolution of ± 0.001 
C

claims.claim: The FLIR SC650 thermal imaging camera recorded thermography at a frequency of 30 Hz.
claims.quote:
FLIR  SC650  thermal  imaging  camera  was  used  to  measure  ther-
mography of the batteries during destructive impact tests at a frequency 
of 30 Hz.

claims.claim: The flammability limits (FLs) of battery vent gas (BVG) were measured using a 5 L spherical explosive device.
claims.quote:
the flammability limits (FLs) of battery vent gas (BVG) were measured by a 5 L spherical explosive 
device.

claims.claim: Flammability limit (FL) is defined as the critical fuel concentration required for flame propagation and is categorized into UFL and LFL.
claims.quote:
The FL is 
defined as the critical fuel concentration required for flame propagation 
and is categorized into the upper flammability limit (UFL) and the lower 
flammability limit (LFL)

claims.claim: A mixed gas can be considered ignited when the internal pressure of the explosion vessel increases by 7%.
claims.quote:
It is found that when 
the internal pressure of the explosion vessel increases by 7 %, it can be 
considered that the mixed gas is ignited.

claims.claim: At initial temperature 300 K and ambient pressure 100 kPa, increasing H2 volume concentration from 10% to 30% decreased LFL from 15.65% to 12.3% and increased UFL from 50.6% to 56.92%.
claims.quote:
The initial 
temperature is 300 K and the ambient pressure is 100 k Pa. In Fig. 3(a), 
the FLs of BVG are presented for variations in H2 volume concentration.
... As the H2  volume concentration increases from 10 % to 30 %, the LFL 
decreases from 15.65 % to 12.3 %, while the UFL increases from 50.6 % 
to 56.92 %.

claims.claim: At initial temperature 300 K and ambient pressure 100 kPa, increasing CO concentration from 5% to 50% decreased LFL from 14.8% to 12.71% and increased UFL from 44.85% to 54.6%.
claims.quote:
The initial 
temperature is 300 K and the ambient pressure is 100 k Pa.
... As the CO concentration increases from 5 
% to 50 %, the LFL decreases from 14.8 % to 12.71 %. Meanwhile, the 
UFL  exhibits  an  opposite  trend,  increasing  from  44.85  %  to  54.6  %.

claims.claim: CuO decoration decreases the InSe band gap from 1.938 eV to 0.831 eV.
claims.quote: CuO decoration decreases  the  InSe  band  gap  from  1.938  eV  to  0.831  eV

claims.claim: A negative binding energy value suggests that decoration is thermodynamically and structurally stable, and greater negativity indicates higher stability.
claims.quote: By analyzing the calculation results of Eq. (1), a negative value suggests that the decoration is thermodynam-ically and structurally stable, with greater negativity indicating higher stability

claims.claim: The final binding energy Eb of InSe decorated with CuO is −2.133 eV.
claims.quote: the final  binding  energy  Eb  of  InSe decorated CuO is obtained to be (cid:0) 2.133 eV

claims.claim: AIMD simulations were performed on InSe monolayer and CuO-InSe monolayer at 300 K and 400 K.
claims.quote: Ab initio molecular dynamics (AIMD) simulations are performed on InSe monolayer and CuO-InSe monolayer at 300 K and 400 K.

claims.claim: For insulating or semiconducting systems, the band gap is defined as the energy difference between the CBM (lowest energy above the Fermi level) and the VBM (highest energy below the Fermi level).
claims.quote: For  insulating  or  semiconducting  systems,  the  valence  band  maximum (VBM) is identified as the highest energy point below the Fermi level in the  electronic  band  structure,  while  the  conduction  band  minimum (CBM) corresponds to the lowest energy point above the Fermi level. The energy difference between the CBM and the VBM represents the band gap

claims.claim: The study compared three heating techniques—heating rods, coils, and plates—on thermal runaway and gas generation in a commercially used NCM811 lithium-ion battery with an energy density of 280.24 Wh/kg (46950 model).
claims.quote: This study compares the impacts of three heating techniques—heating rods, coils, and plates—on thermal runaway and gas generation in a commercially used NCM811 lithium-ion battery, which has a high energy density of 280.24 Wh/kg (the latest cylindrical 46950 model).

claims.claim: Thermal runaway was triggered after 225 s of heating with the heating coil.
claims.quote: Specifically, thermal runaway is triggered after 225 s of heating with the coil,

claims.claim: The heating coil triggered thermal runaway at 140.07 °C, which was 48.94 °C and 38.06 °C higher than the temperatures triggered by the rod and plate, respectively.
claims.quote:
reaching 140.07
C, which is 48.94
C and 38.06
C higher than the temperatures triggered by the rod and plate, respectively.

claims.claim: Successful airtightness was confirmed if the chamber sustained 20 atm for 10 min with a leakage rate not exceeding 1 kPa/min.
claims.quote: Successful airtightness is confirmed if the chamber sustains 20 atm for 10 min with a leakage rate not exceeding 1 kpa/min;

claims.claim: Each thermal runaway test heated at a constant power of 265 W until the battery entered thermal runaway, triggering the heating to stop.
claims.quote:
Initiate the heater to heat each experiment at a constant power
of 265 W until the battery enters thermal runaway triggering the heating to stop.

claims.claim: The study comparatively evaluated four thermal insulation materials (TIMs): mica plate, SiAG pre-oxidized fiber, aluminosilicate ceramic fiber, and nano SiAG felt.
claims.quote: This study comparatively evaluated four distinct thermal insulation materials  (TIMs): mica  plate,  SiAG pre-oxidized  fiber, aluminosilicate  ceramic fiber, and  nano  SiAG  felt

claims.claim: The effectiveness of each TIM was assessed using temperature profiles (ΔT), toxic gas emissions (CO, NO2, SO2), and structural/thermal stability (SEM, TGA).
claims.quote: The effectiveness of each TIM was assessed by temperature profiles (ΔT), toxic gas emissions (CO, NO2, SO2), and structural/thermal stability (SEM, TGA).

claims.claim: Mica plate, SiAG pre-oxidized fiber, and nano SiAG felt effectively inhibited thermal runaway propagation to Cells 2–4, while no insulation and aluminosilicate ceramic fiber failed and led to combustion of all Cells 1–4.
claims.quote: The results, presented in Fig. 3, demonstrate that mica plate, SiAG pre-oxidized fiber, and nano SiAG felt effectively inhibited TR propagation to Cells 2–4. In contrast, the absence of insulation and the use of aluminosilicate ceramic fiber failed to prevent propagation, leading to the combustion of all Cells 1–4.

claims.claim: The temperature difference used to evaluate TIM n was defined as ΔTTIM n = Tn,b max − Tn+1,f max.
claims.quote:
The temperature difference ΔTTIM  n is employed to evaluate the capacity of TIM n to mitigate TRP. 

ΔTTIM n = Tn,b max (cid:0) Tn+1,f max

claims.quote: The external heater all provided the same (1.03 W/cm2) heating power density to the three different heating positions of LIBs

claims.claim: Thermal runaway occurrence was determined by Th temperature rise rate exceeding 1 ℃/s, mass loss rate continuing to exceed 0.5 g/s, and a considerable amount of white smoke ejected from the safety valve.
claims.quote: In this work, the occurrence of TR was determined as the tempera- ture rise rate of Th  exceeding 1 ℃/s, the mass loss rate of the battery continuing to exceed 0.5 g/s and a considerable amount of white smoke is ejected from the safety valve.

claims.claim: The flame-retardant phase change material (FRPCM) selected for TRP suppression experiments had mass ratios of 63% PA, 7% EG, 3% kaolin, and 27% IFR.
claims.quote:
the PCMs with mass ratios
of 63% (PA), 7% (EG), 3% (kaolin), and 27% (IFR) were chosen for the

claims.claim: The study introduces a reduced-order thermal runaway network (TRN) model by redistributing heat source terms and correcting thermal runaway trigger criteria.
claims.quote: This study introduces a highly accurate reduced-order thermal runaway network (TRN) model by redistributing heat source terms and correcting thermal runaway trigger criteria.

claims.claim: Compared to traditional thermal network models, the TRN model ensures the accuracy of thermal runaway trigger time to within 1 min.
claims.quote: Compared to traditional thermal network models, the TRN model attains precise simulation of thermal runaway propagation, ensuring the accuracy of thermal runaway trigger time to within 1 min.

claims.claim: The thermal runaway propagation interval is reduced by 72% from 611 s to 176 s.
claims.quote: Specifically, the thermal runaway propagation interval is markedly reduced by 72%, from 611 s to 176 s, indicating that controlling the initial thermal spread within the module is more critical than mitigating thermal propagation between modules.

claims.claim: During thermal runaway propagation, energy dissipated via the liquid cooling plates accounts for more than 70% of the total energy.
claims.quote: Moreover, during thermal runaway propagation, the energy dissipated via the liquid cooling plates accounts for more than 70% of the total energy, far exceeding the heat transfer through neighboring battery surfaces and the tab connector.

claims.claim: In the TRN model, the temperature at the initial failure position is expressed as Tfl = η • Tcenter.
claims.quote:
Tfl is expressed as,

Tfl = η • Tcenter

claims.claim: The study used commercial 21700 lithium-ion batteries with an NMC cathode from Tesla Model 3 EVs, with dimensions 21 mm in diameter and 70 mm in height.
claims.quote: Commercial 21700 LIBs of lithium nickel manganese cobalt oxide (NMC) cathode (Tesla Model 3 EVs, 21 mm in diameter and 70 mm in height) were used in this work

claims.claim: The battery had a rated capacity of 5 Ah and rated voltage of 3.7 V.
claims.quote: This battery has a rated capacity and voltage of 5 Ah and 3.7 V

claims.claim: The battery had a lower voltage limit of 2.75 V and an upper voltage limit of 4.2 V.
claims.quote: with a lower and upper voltage limit of 2.75 and 4.2 V, respectively

claims.claim: The low-pressure chamber used in the experiments had an internal volume of 12 m3 and could provide pressures from 15 to 101 kPa.
claims.quote: A chamber of an internal volume 12 m3 was designed to provide a well-controlled environment (pressures vary from 15 to 101 kPa)

claims.claim: The experiments considered environmental pressures of 101, 90, 70, 50, and 30 kPa.
claims.quote: In this work, 101, 90, 70, 50, and 30 kPa were considered

claims.claim: The battery cell is modeled as a heat source at 500 K.
claims.quote: The battery cell, which acts as a heat source with a temperature of 500 K

claims.claim: The outer wall of PCM2 is specified as a cold source with an ambient temperature of 300 K.
claims.quote: The outer wall of PCM2 is specified as a cold source with an ambient temperature of 300 K.

claims.claim: PCM1 is RT82 and PCM2 is RT27 in the trans-radial arrangement.
claims.quote: The PCM closest to the battery cell is referred to as RT82, and the PCM in the outermost layer is known as RT27.

claims.claim: The diameters of PCM1 and PCM2 are 30 mm and 42 mm, respectively.
claims.quote: The diameters of PCM1 and PCM2 are 30 mm and 42 mm, respectively.

claims.claim: For RT82 PCM with 6% SWCNT nanoparticles, the melting temperature distribution increases by up to 15.27% compared to pure PCM.
claims.quote: For RT82 PCM with 6 % SWCNT nanoparticles, the melting temperature distribution in-creases by up to 15.27 % compared to the pure PCM scenario.

claims.claim: The study evaluated thermal runaway and gas generation of the latest cylindrical 46950 NCM811 lithium-ion battery with an energy density of 280.24 Wh/kg.
claims.quote: This study compares the impacts of three heating techniques—heating rods, coils, and plates—on thermal runaway and gas generation in a commercially used NCM811 lithium-ion battery, which has a high energy density of 280.24 Wh/kg (the latest cylindrical 46950 model).

claims.claim: Thermal runaway was triggered after 225 s of heating with the coil.
claims.quote: Specifically, thermal runaway is triggered after 225 s of heating with the coil

claims.claim: The heating coil method showed the highest peak gas generation at 7.72 mol.
claims.quote: The heating coil method shows the highest peak gas generation at 7.72 mol

claims.claim: At 100% SOC, the descending order of CO2 concentrations was heating coil (27.73%), heating plate (24.6%), and heating rod (18.08%).
claims.quote: According to the test results shown in Fig. 7, under the same 100 % SOC conditions, the descending order of CO2 gas concentrations is as follows: heating coil (27.73 %), heating plate (24.6 %), and heating rod (18.08 %).

claims.claim: The flammable concentration ranges (largest to smallest) were heating rod (51.11%), heating coil (49.82%), and heating plate (46.94%).
claims.quote: As shown in Fig. 9, the flammable concentration ranges from largest to smallest for the three heating methods is: heating rod (51.11 %), heating coil (49.82 %), and heating plate (46.94 %).

claims.claim: The thermal behavior of pouch lithium-ion capacitors was investigated at transient high discharge rates from 1C to 550C.
claims.quote: The thermal behavior of pouch lithium-ion capacitors in this work is systematically investigated at transient high- rates from 1C to 550C.

claims.claim: As the discharge rate increases, the temperature curve shows an upward parabolic trend at the end of discharge, reaching a maximum temperature of 33.68 C at 200C.
claims.quote:
As  the  discharge  rate  increases,  the  temperature  curve  shows  an  upward  parabolic  trend at  the  end  of  discharge,  with  a maximum  temperature  of  33.68 
C at  200C.

claims.claim: At 550C, the proportion of irreversible heat to total heat generation is close to 80%.
claims.quote:
The pro-
portion of irreversible heat to total heat generation is close to 80 % at 550C

claims.claim: At 550C, the temperature at the end of discharge decreases because of reduced discharge time.
claims.quote:
but the temperature at the end of 
discharge decreases owing to the reduced discharge time.

claims.claim: The intermittent current method's calculated temperature trends are in better agreement with measured values than other methods because polarization internal resistance is significantly related to the discharge time interval.
claims.quote:
It can be found that the intermittent current method calculated 
temperature  trends  are  in  good  agreement  with  the  measured  values  better  than  other  methods,  because  the 
polarization internal resistance is significantly related to the discharge time interval.
